name: Lectoure and Plieux – loop in the little French Tuscany
name_original: Lectoure et Plieux – boucle dans la petite Toscane française
region_intro: Touring cycling around Lectoure is characterized by its gently undulating countryside, often referred to as "French Tuscany." The region features rolling hills, expansive vineyards, and fields of wheat and sunflowers, creating a varied landscape for cyclists. The Gers Valley offers panoramic views, and the area is traversed by the Gers and Auroue rivers. This topography provides a mix of moderate and challenging routes for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Lectoure The most popular touring cycling route is Lectoure - Fleurance - Pauilhac Loop, a 19.9 miles (32.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through charming villages and scenic countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lectoure, La Romieu et Marsolan – loop in the little French Tuscany, a difficult 29.9 miles (48.2 km) path. This route features rolling hills and historical sites, including the UNESCO-listed Collegiate Church of Saint Pierre in La Romieu. Local touring cyclists also love the Lectoure and Plieux – loop in the little French Tuscany, a 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail leading through picturesque countryside and vineyards, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Lectoure is defined by rolling hills, vineyards, and fields of wheat and sunflowers. The network offers options for moderate to difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels. The routes in Lectoure are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 49 reviews. More than 252 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lectoure's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of touring cycling routes can I find around Lectoure?
A: Lectoure offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, characterized by its gently undulating countryside, often referred to as "French Tuscany." You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels. The terrain includes rolling hills, expansive vineyards, and fields of wheat and sunflowers, providing scenic views throughout your ride.

Q: Are there many touring cycling routes in the Lectoure area?
A: Yes, there are over 35 touring cycling routes available around Lectoure. These routes vary in length and difficulty, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What are some popular circular touring cycling routes in Lectoure?
A: Many routes around Lectoure are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021112658/lectoure-fleurance-pauilhac-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure - Fleurance - Pauilhac Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 32 km long. For a more challenging ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021110751/lectoure-la-romieu-et-marsolan-loop-in-the-little-french-tuscany" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure, La Romieu et Marsolan – loop in the little French Tuscany&lt;/a&gt;, covering nearly 50 km and featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes near Lectoure?
A: While many routes feature rolling hills, some moderate options can be suitable for families with older children or those using electric bikes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26422111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure – Lake of the Three Valleys loop from Cloutats&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22.5 km route that passes by Lac des 3 Vallées, offering opportunities for picnicking and other activities, making it a good choice for a family outing.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Lectoure and its surroundings are rich in history. Many routes pass through charming villages and historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021110751/lectoure-la-romieu-et-marsolan-loop-in-the-little-french-tuscany" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure, La Romieu et Marsolan – loop in the little French Tuscany&lt;/a&gt; takes you to La Romieu, home to the UNESCO World Heritage-listed &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3903532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Saint Pierre&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter various castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5859953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Madirac&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5681633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Terraube&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the region's rich heritage.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Lectoure?
A: Lectoure benefits from a mild climate, making spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, the weather is generally pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant with blooming flowers or autumnal colors. Summer can also be enjoyable, but it's advisable to start early to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region's rolling hills provide numerous vantage points. From the Bastion Promenade in Lectoure itself, you can enjoy panoramic vistas of the Gers Valley. Many cycling routes traverse elevated areas, offering similar sweeping views of the countryside, including fields of melon, wheat, and sunflowers, and on clear days, even glimpses of the Pyrenees.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Lectoure?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lectoure are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 49 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque countryside, the charming villages, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with other outdoor activities in Lectoure?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities to combine cycling with other activities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26422111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure – Lake of the Three Valleys loop from Cloutats&lt;/a&gt; passes by Lac des 3 Vallées, where you can enjoy fishing, boating, or picnicking. Additionally, Lectoure lies along the Via Podiensis, part of the Way of St. James pilgrimage route, attracting both cyclists and hikers.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through vineyards or agricultural fields?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Lectoure will take you through the heart of Gascony's agricultural landscape. You'll frequently pass by expansive vineyards, as well as fields of wheat, sunflowers, and melons, which are characteristic of the region and offer beautiful scenery, especially during their respective seasons.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling in Lectoure?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lectoure range from moderate to difficult. While there are no easy routes listed, the moderate options are manageable for regular cyclists, often featuring rolling hills. The difficult routes involve more significant elevation changes and longer distances, suitable for those seeking a greater challenge. Electric bike assistance can make some of the more challenging routes more accessible.
tips: The town of Lectoure is located on an old oppidum, so it is visible from afar. It is located in a part of the Gers department nicknamed "French Tuscany". No less than ten buildings within the walls of the old town are on the list of historical monuments.

Small village, in the heart of French Tosacne, which houses an imposing 14th century castle, which has become a center for contemporary art exhibitions and cultural encounters. It notably houses the works of the artist Jean-Paul Marcheschi
